title: dactylorchis incarnata var. divaricata
intro: Dactylorchis incarnata var. divaricata, also known as the lesser twayblade, is a type of orchid native to Northern Hemisphere regions, particularly found in wet meadows and bogs. The lesser twayblade has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
